preamble: The shift from the labiodental fricative [f] to a glottal stop [ʔ] is a severe form of debuccalization. In this process, the speaker abandons the oral gesture (lip to teeth) entirely, replacing the consonant with a simple glottal closure, typically in rapid or informal speech.

examples.note: In syllable-coda positions, voiceless fricatives like [f] can be replaced by a glottal stop in rapid, informal speech.
In dialects that debuccalize /f/ to [h], the resulting aspiration can sometimes harden into a glottal stop before a glide.
